title: Festuca genuensis
intro: Festuca genuensis, also known as Italian fescue or Festuca altaica, is a species of grass belonging to the family Poaceae, commonly found in the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are said to aid in reducing stress and promoting overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Festuca genuensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and phenolic glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and dried extracts, which are often used to support respiratory health and to treat conditions such as bronchitis and asthma.
taxonomy: Festuca genuensis is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Festuca, which comprises various grass species. Within the genus Festuca, F. genuensis is often grouped under the subgenus Festuca. The exact classification of F. genuensis may vary depending on the source, but it is generally considered to be a part of the kingdom Plantae. Further classification details can be found in the most recent taxonomic studies on the species.
